title: caralluma carinata
intro: Caralluma carinata, also known as 'Vrikshamla', is a succulent plant native to the tropical regions of India and Sri Lanka. It has been used for centuries in traditional Ayurvedic medicine, with medicinal properties attributed to its ability to inhibit the production of an enzyme called citrate lyase, which is involved in the conversion of carbohydrates into fat. The health benefits of Caralluma carinata include weight loss, improved glucose metabolism, and reduced inflammation, making it a potential natural remedy for managing obesity and diabetes. Caralluma carinata is often prepared in herbal supplements, teas, and extracts, which are available in various forms for those looking to harness its medicinal properties.
